human_verification.notes: Reviewed transmission-specific boundary controls, outage and reservation artifact handling, RC/TOP/BA/TSP owner mapping, source coverage, scenario-to-ability mapping, and refusal posture for switching, approval, tariff, engineering, and compliance authority.

review_notes: All required issue scenarios and research-contract scenario families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required shared, coordination-handoffs-and-routing, and cross-industry abilities are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls keep the lane on transmission coordination, routing, and blocker management rather than switching, outage approval, dispatch control, tariff interpretation, engineering review, or compliance signoff.
Authority sources cover FERC transmission-access context, NERC role and TOP obligations, NAESB WEQ workflow references, and NERC terminology with explicit refresh and decay rules.

Scenario coverage includes missing prerequisites, duplicate ownership, conflicting-source handling, adjacent-lane routing, transmission terminology handling, and closure refusal when artifact traces are incomplete.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit documentation for any later retained-implementation promotion.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; regional and tenant-specific differences are explicitly handled through retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated use as a specification-based transmission coordination overlay.
